skill.name: schedule-management
skill.description: Local schedule management for adding events, tracking deadlines, generating reminders, and detecting time conflicts when users need offline scheduling with optional popup notifications.
skill.summary: Local schedule management tool for adding events, tracking deadlines, detecting conflicts, and generating reminders without cloud services.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 81 potential issues with risk score 100/100, but review confirms nearly all are false positives from markdown code blocks and documentation content. The skill is a legitimate local-only schedule management CLI tool with no network access, no credential handling, and no obfuscated code. The only confirmed risk factor is external_commands, which is expected for a Python CLI tool that runs scripts on the local machine. The legacy audit (schedule-management_audit_result_v1.json) graded this skill as Production Ready with score 87/100.

medium_findings.title: PowerShell ExecutionPolicy Bypass documented
medium_findings.description: Documentation shows running PowerShell with ExecutionPolicy Bypass flag. While this is intended usage for the notification feature, bypassing execution policy lowers Windows security controls. User must run this command manually.

low_findings.title: Scheduled task creation documented
low_findings.description: Documentation includes schtasks /Create command for periodic reminder checks. This is a documentation example that requires manual execution. No auto-creation of scheduled tasks occurs in the skill code.

low_findings.title: Source URL in documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded GitHub URL in SKILL.md line 7 points to the source repository. This is a documentation reference link, not a network call. The skill has no actual network capability.

content.user_title: Manage schedules and track deadlines offline
content.value_statement: Users need lightweight offline schedule management without cloud sync or complex tools. This skill provides local event storage, deadline tracking, conflict detection, and optional desktop reminders using simple Python scripts.
actual_capabilities: Add events and deadlines with titles, times, locations, and notes
List and filter stored events by time range
Detect overlapping schedule conflicts on the same date
Generate reminder exports for upcoming events
Show desktop popup notifications for reminders on Windows
limitations: No cloud sync or multi-device sharing across computers
Desktop popup notifications require Windows with PowerShell
Periodic reminders need manual scheduled task setup
No integration with external calendar services like Google Calendar

use_cases.title: Offline deadline management
use_cases.description: Track project deadlines and milestones without connecting to any external service or cloud.

use_cases.title: Schedule conflict checking
use_cases.description: Check for overlapping meetings and events before committing to new time slots.
use_cases.target_user: Small team leads and project coordinators

use_cases.title: Local event record keeping
use_cases.description: Store and organize events in a simple local directory for environments with restricted network access.

prompt_templates.title: Add a new event
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a meeting called Team Standup tomorrow from 9:00 to 9:30 with a reminder 15 minutes before.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create a new event in the local schedule store

prompt_templates.title: Check for scheduling conflicts
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my schedule for any overlapping events this week.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to detect time conflicts before planning new events

prompt_templates.title: Import events from a file
prompt_templates.prompt: Import events from my existing CSV file at ./backup/events.csv into my schedule.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to bulk import events from a CSV export

prompt_templates.title: Set up reminders and notifications
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate reminders for all upcoming events and configure desktop popup notifications.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to receive desktop notifications for upcoming events

output_examples.input: Add a team standup meeting tomorrow at 9 AM for 30 minutes.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a new event entry in the local events file with the title, start time, end time, and type set to meeting. The event is available for listing and conflict checking.

output_examples.input: Check if I have any scheduling conflicts this week.
output_examples.output: The skill scans all stored events for overlapping time windows on the same date. Any conflicting event pairs are identified with their titles and time ranges.

output_examples.input: Set up reminder notifications for my upcoming deadlines.
output_examples.output: The skill generates a reminder CSV file with all upcoming events that need attention. On Windows, the PowerShell notifier can show desktop popup messages for each reminder.

best_practices: Use the required time format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM consistently for all events to prevent parsing errors.
Run conflict detection before adding new events to identify and resolve scheduling overlaps.
Set up the notifier script as a scheduled task on Windows for automatic periodic reminder notifications.
anti_patterns: Do not edit events.jsonl or reminders.csv files manually. Always use the tool operations to modify data.
Do not store sensitive information in event titles or notes. Data is stored as plain text in local files.
Do not share the data directory across different versions of the tool. File format may change between versions.

faq.question: Does this skill sync with my online calendar?
faq.answer: No. This tool is local-only and does not connect to any external service or cloud platform.

faq.question: Can I use this skill on macOS or Linux?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python schedule script runs on any operating system. Desktop popup notifications require Windows.

faq.question: Where are my events stored?
faq.answer: Events are stored in a local directory you specify. Files include events.jsonl, reminders.csv, and notified.log.

faq.question: How do I enable popup notifications?
faq.answer: Generate reminders using the tool, then run the PowerShell notifier script on Windows. You can automate this with Task Scheduler.

faq.question: Can I import events from another system?
faq.answer: Yes. You can import events from a CSV file using the import operation with the correct column headers.

faq.question: What happens with invalid time formats?
faq.answer: The tool rejects invalid formats and shows a clear error message. Accepted format is YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM in 24-hour time.
